pattern: The corner-to-corner walker's movement rule pins its path to a single fixed diagonal, which frees the other two walkers into two independent shortest-path-style sweeps that only ever need to avoid double-counting where they cross that diagonal.
hints: Work out exactly which cells the walker starting at the top-left corner is forced to visit -- its movement options only allow one possible route to the opposite corner.
For each of the other two walkers, run a row-by-row (or column-by-column) sweep tracking the best total reachable at every position, choosing among its three allowed moves at each step.
Zero out the forced walker's cells before running the other two sweeps, then add the forced walker's own total back in once -- that avoids paying for any cell twice.

edge_inputs.description: 3x3 grid where the richest cells sit off the forced diagonal
4x4 grid with one very valuable corner cell shared by all three paths

stress.description: random small-to-medium grids with modest fruit counts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 60))
    fruits = [[rng.randint(0, 50) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [fruits]
